title: A Valentine by Edgar Allan Poe — Love Poetry Lines
content:
For her this rhyme is penned, whose luminous eyes,
  Brightly expressive as the twins of Leda,
Shall find her own sweet name, that, nestling lies
  Upon the page, enwrapped from every reader.
Search narrowly the lines!--they hold a treasure
  Divine--a talisman--an amulet
That must be worn _at heart_. Search well the measure--
  The words--the syllables! Do not forget
meaning: "A Valentine" by Edgar Allan Poe is a love and deep and nature poem consisting of 20 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "For her this rhyme is penned, whose luminous eyes,   Brightly expressive as the twins of Leda,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Edgar Allan Poe's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
